opp.ais_why:
• Dec 2025 / Jan 2026 Avoma notes show PV Group buyer Vincent focused on cost-effective S2P (verbatim framing 'not Rolls Royce'), DORA compliance, SAP S/4HANA integration and RFP shortlist construction. Caroline Lacocque pitched AI agents in the conversations but the customer never engaged on that topic in any of the 3 meetings ‚Äî every customer reply pivoted back to cost, DORA regulation, or SAP integration.
• Product scope is purely traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSave, iSource, iSupplier, iCompliance) with zero AI products in scope ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Merlin Intake, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze. 3 Avoma meetings with up to a 15-person customer team confirm traditional procurement evaluation with a financial-services regulatory focus.
• High confidence: call transcripts plus product list are both consistent and reinforcing. The customer is a budget-conscious financial-services-regulator-driven buyer; AI is not on their selection criteria despite Zycus rep-side AI pitches. Score 2 reflects active rejection of the AI narrative in favour of cost + DORA compliance + SAP integration.

He is engaged (discovery call 2025-12-08, demo 2026-01-21, portal access request 2026-03-31), has placed Zycus on the RFP shortlist, and stated on 2025-12-08 'I'm already convinced that you might be a good supplier for me... you are in my shortlist'. Evidence of advocacy: Vincent explicitly excluded high-cost competitors ('I'm not looking for rolls rolls... not SAP Ariba, it's a dinosaur, we don't need this, and we won't pay for such a solution') and stated affordability and composability favor Zycus; he accepted the Vienna Horizon event invitation from Caroline Lacocque on 2025-12-08 ('With pleasure. However, Vincent is working solo on the RFP ('unfortunately, I'm quite alone to handle the job'), and no other PV Group stakeholder (legal, finance, business users, IT) has engaged on any call. Champion strength is DEVELOPING: Vincent is bought in and driving the technical evaluation, but he has not yet multi-threaded the relationship (no introductions to the EB, DM, legal, or other influencers), has not provided internal advocacy evidence (shared docs, pushed back on competitors in writing, driven the agenda beyond his own scope), and engagement is thinning (last call 2026-01-21, ~5 months ago; only a portal-access note 2026-03-31 since then, ~3 months ago). The champion is ACTIVE but not yet STRONG; securing Vincent's deeper advocacy (written endorsement, EB introduction, internal selling) and multi-threading to other stakeholders are the top near-term moves.

competition.narrative: Vincent Buelens stated on 2025-12-08 that Zycus is in a competitive RFP shortlist of max 3–4 suppliers from an initial pool of ~5 competitors. Named competitors: Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP (inferred from Vincent's reference to 'well known' competitors and Caroline's competitive positioning). Vincent explicitly downgraded Coupa and SAP Ariba on 2025-12-08: 'I'm not looking for rolls rolls because I don't have the budget... SAP Ariba, that's absolutely not what I'm looking for because it's too broad... it's a dinosaur. We don't need this, and we won't pay for such a solution. No.' Caroline Lacocque (Zycus owner, ex-Coupa) reinforced on the same call: 'Coupa criticized for heavy marketing, partner reliance, private equity ownership, support issues, and higher cost' and 'Ivalua's single-tenant model requires change orders for customizations and complicates upgrades'. Vincent did not name the other 3–4 shortlist finalists explicitly, but the competitive wedge is clear: Zycus is positioned as affordable, privately owned, composable (start anywhere / phased approach), with out-of-box S/4HANA integration and strong AI roadmap, versus Coupa (expensive, support issues, private-equity churn) and Ivalua (single-tenant, change-order costs). The competitive landscape is Coupa/Ivalua as the known enterprise peers (high threat on brand, lowered on price/fit per Vincent's feedback) and GEP as the configure-to-order alternative; Zycus is the mid-market composable challenger. The shortlist down-select and RFP scoring are the next competitive gates; reinforcing the affordability + S/4HANA + DORA compliance wedge, and demonstrating customer references (financial services: Bank of Cyprus, UOB, Mitsubishi per Caroline's offer 2025-12-08) are the recommended competitive moves.

identify_pain.narrative: Vincent Buelens owns the pain and articulated it clearly on 2025-12-08. Operational pain: PV Group has no procurement tool, all contract and spend management is manual ('we are handling all the business and all the contracts manually. It's quite a mess'), no centralized supplier or contract repository, no visibility into spend, no analytics or dashboards ('you cannot look for savings'), fragmented processes, high manual workload for non-strategic transactions. Financial pain: Vincent stated 'I already know that I'm paying way too much for a lot of contracts' — acknowledged cost leakage across ~€100–150M indirect spend but no quantified amount. Compliance pain: as a regulated financial institution, PV Group must meet DORA requirements (annual supplier criticality classification, contract review, CSV reporting to the relevant stakeholder of Belgium), currently managed manually. Urgency: Vincent was hired Dec 2025 to replace a manager who left 6 months prior; the project had been approved and funded but was on hold, and implementing the tool is his 'top priority' ('that's my first priority for next year') with a hard deadline (2027-01-01 parallel S/4HANA go-live). The pain is confirmed, urgent, and owned by the procurement manager; the broader stakeholder pain (legal, finance, business users) has not been articulated on calls yet.

decision_criteria.narrative: Vincent Buelens stated the evaluation criteria across two calls. On 2025-12-08 he specified: (1) full S2P scope (PR request → approval workflow → sourcing/RFP → contract award → invoicing, integrated end-to-end); (2) out-of-box SAP S/4HANA API connector for parallel 2027-01-01 go-live ('the goal for us is to match it with the future procurement tool in 2027, first January'); (3) Peppol API integration for AP automation by January; (4) DORA compliance (PV Group is a regulated financial institution under National Bank of Belgium oversight) — tool must extract contract reference, annual budget, consumption, criticality classification (critical/non-critical) to CSV for annual NBB reporting; (5) affordability ('not looking for rolls rolls... I'm looking for a good solution, but also affordable'); (6) ease of adoption ('people don't know how to manage... I will introduce, implement everything, and teach the people the mindset'). On 2026-01-21 the deep-dive demo validated fit against these criteria: accounting module, SAP integration, DORA compliance module, contract management — feedback 'well received' per Next_Step. The shortlist criteria are clear and documented; Zycus is positioned as meeting them (S/4HANA connector confirmed, DORA compliance demoed, composable/phased pricing). Formal fit/gap and BRD alignment are next milestones.

best_practice_check.summary: PV Group is a live, funded, stage-appropriate deal (Formal Evaluation, 277 days to 2027-03-30 close, last activity 3 days ago). Three win-strategy levers demand focus NOW: (1) Multi-thread beyond Vincent — he is solo and bought-in but has not introduced the Economic Buyer (CFO/De Troch), legal, finance, or business users; a single-threaded champion in a regulated financial institution is a political and approval risk. Map and engage the EB, legal (for MSA/DORA), and at least one business-user influencer in the next 14 days. (2) Quantify the business case — Vincent acknowledged cost leakage ('paying way too much') but no ROI/savings target exists; a €100–150M spend base with no quantified value case is a pricing/justification vulnerability when the EB enters. Co-build a savings model (top-20-supplier rate-card benchmark, manual-workload-hours saved, DORA-compliance-effort reduction) with Vincent before the RFP scoring gate. (3) Reinforce the Ivalua wedge — Ivalua is the strongest CURRENT threat (enterprise S2P peer, financial-services traction) but exposed on single-tenant change-order costs and flexibility vs Zycus composable/phased model. Vincent is affordability-focused; deliver the financial-services references (Bank of Cyprus, UOB, Mitsubishi offered 2025-12-08), demonstrate the S/4HANA connector + DORA compliance depth, and convert Vincent's positive sentiment ('well received' demo 2026-01-21) into written advocacy (internal champion memo, EB intro) before the RFP down-select.

competitive_position.summary: PV Group is running a formal RFP with a shortlist of max 3–4 suppliers from ~5 initial competitors. Named/inferred rivals: Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP. Recency-weighted threat assessment as of 2026-06-26: Coupa and Ivalua are the ACTIVE enterprise peers on the shortlist but buyer Vincent Buelens explicitly downgraded both on 2025-12-08 (Coupa = expensive + support issues + private-equity risk; SAP Ariba = 'dinosaur', too broad, priced out; Ivalua = single-tenant change-order costs). GEP is inferred as the configure-to-order alternative. The SINGLE strongest CURRENT threat is IVALUA: a known S2P enterprise platform with financial-services traction, competing on completeness and maturity, but exposed on price/flexibility (single-tenant model, change orders) per Caroline's positioning 2025-12-08. Coupa is a LOWER current threat: Vincent stated on 2025-12-08 'we won't pay for such a solution' and Caroline (ex-Coupa) reinforced support/cost weaknesses. SAP Ariba is DORMANT (priced out, 'dinosaur'). Zycus wedge: affordable + composable + out-of-box S/4HANA connector + DORA compliance + privately owned + strong AI roadmap. The competitive gate is RFP scoring and shortlist down-select; reinforcing the affordability + S/4HANA + DORA fit and delivering financial-services references (Bank of Cyprus, UOB, Mitsubishi offered 2025-12-08) are the activation moves.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: PV Group's stated requirements align well with Zycus capabilities demonstrated on 2025-12-08 and 2026-01-21. ALIGNED: (1) Full S2P scope (sourcing, contract, invoicing, eProcurement) — Zycus composable platform confirmed. (2) Out-of-box SAP S/4HANA API connector for 2027-01-01 parallel go-live — confirmed available. (3) DORA compliance (contract criticality, CSV export for National Bank of Belgium) — demoed 2026-01-21, 'well received'. (4) Affordability and composability — Zycus positioned as mid-market vs Coupa/Ivalua. PARTIALLY ALIGNED: (5) Peppol API integration for AP automation by January 2027 — flagged as requirement 2025-12-08 ('we have to find a solution which can make the API with Paypal. From January'), but Peppol certification/Access Point details were requested on 2026-01-21 demo and not resolved on that call; follow-up documentation pending. (6) Ease of adoption for non-technical users ('people don't know how to manage... I will teach the people') — Merlin Intake conversational UI demoed 2025-12-08 as adoption accelerator, but no user acceptance testing or stakeholder validation beyond Vincent yet. EXPOSED: No other PV Group stakeholder (legal, finance, IT, business users) has validated fit on any call; Vincent is solo. Multi-threading and stakeholder validation are the next fit milestones.

hard.ais_why:
• Dec 2025 / Jan 2026 Avoma notes show PV Group buyer Vincent focused on cost-effective S2P (verbatim framing 'not Rolls Royce'), DORA compliance, SAP S/4HANA integration and RFP shortlist construction. Caroline Lacocque pitched AI agents in the conversations but the customer never engaged on that topic in any of the 3 meetings ‚Äî every customer reply pivoted back to cost, DORA regulation, or SAP integration.
• Product scope is purely traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSave, iSource, iSupplier, iCompliance) with zero AI products in scope ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Merlin Intake, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze. 3 Avoma meetings with up to a 15-person customer team confirm traditional procurement evaluation with a financial-services regulatory focus.
• High confidence: call transcripts plus product list are both consistent and reinforcing. The customer is a budget-conscious financial-services-regulator-driven buyer; AI is not on their selection criteria despite Zycus rep-side AI pitches. Score 2 reflects active rejection of the AI narrative in favour of cost + DORA compliance + SAP integration.
